MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Req. To Join Estate Of Peter George Euteneuer)
Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JESSE WELLEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (JOINDER) is Off calendar. The court did not receive a courtesy copy of the joinder in compliance with San Francicso Local Rule 2.6B or a proof of service. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Supporting Evidence
Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CLINTON KILLIAN OF FRIED & WILLIAMS LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 625-0100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT ESTATE OF PETER GEORGE EUTENEUER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted in part. The court grants summary judgment in favor of defendant against plaintiff's complaint. First, the undisputed evidence demonstrates that Mr. Holmes assigned the promissory note to plaintiff and plaintiff was a co-obligor on the note. (Defendant's UMF 4). "It has long been established in California that the assignment of a joint and several debt to one of the co-obligors extinguishes that debt. [citations]. The assignment amounts to payment and consequently the evidence of that debt, i.e., the note or judgment, becomes functus officio (of no further effect). [citations] Therefore, no action can be maintained on the original debt." Great Western Bank v. Kong (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 28, 32. Second, plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement that complies with CCP 437c(b)(3). The court denies summary judgment on the cross-complaint because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The motion does not address all of the claims in the cross-complaint, appears to be directed only at the cause of action for cancellation of the instrument, and defendant did not seek summary adjudication in the alternative. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976
